verses.sa:
ॐ प्राच्यां रक्षतु मे तारा कामरूपनिवासिनी ।
आग्नेय्यां षोडशी पातु याम्यां धूमावती स्वयम् ॥१॥
verses.it:
Om prācyāṃ rakṣatu me tārā kāmarūpanivāsinī |
āgneyyāṃ ṣoḍaśī pātu yāmyāṃ dhūmāvatī svayam ||1||
verses.en: May Tārā, who dwells in Kāmarūpa, guard me in the east. In the southeast may Ṣoḍaśī (Tripurasundarī) protect me; in the south, Dhumāvatī herself.
singable: In the east, may Tārā keep me,

verses.sa:
नैरृत्यां भैरवी पातु वारुण्यां भुवनेश्वरी ।
वायव्यां सततं पातु छिन्नमस्ता महेश्वरी ॥२॥
verses.it:
nairṛtyāṃ bhairavī pātu vāruṇyāṃ bhuvaneśvarī |
vāyavyāṃ satataṃ pātu chinnamastā maheśvarī ||2||
verses.en: In the southwest may Bhairavī protect; in the west, Bhuvaneśvarī. In the northwest may the great goddess Chinnamastā protect me always.

verses.sa:
कौबेर्यां पातु मे देवी श्रीविद्या बगलामुखी ।
ऐशान्यां पातु मे नित्यं महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी ॥३॥
verses.it:
kauberyāṃ pātu me devī śrīvidyā bagalāmukhī |
aiśānyāṃ pātu me nityaṃ mahātripurasundarī ||3||
verses.en: In Kubera's quarter (the north) may the goddess Śrīvidyā—Bagalāmukhī—protect me. In Īśāna's quarter (the northeast) may Mahā‑Tripurasundarī protect me always.
singable: the Great One, guards me always.

verses.sa:
ऊर्ध्वं रक्षतु मे विद्या मातङ्गीपीठवासिनी ।
सर्वतः पातु मे नित्यं कामाख्या कालिका स्वयम् ॥४॥
verses.it:
ūrdhvaṃ rakṣatu me vidyā mātaṅgīpīṭhavāsinī |
sarvataḥ pātu me nityaṃ kāmākhyā kālikā svayam ||4||
verses.en: Above, may Vidyā Mātangī, who abides at her sacred seat, guard me. On all sides may Kālikā of Kāmākhyā protect me always.

verses.sa:
ब्रह्मरूपा महाविद्या सर्वविद्यामयी स्वयम् ।
शीर्षे रक्षतु मे दुर्गा भालं श्रीभवगेहिनी ॥५॥
verses.it:
brahmarūpā mahāvidyā sarvavidyāmayī svayam |
śīrṣe rakṣatu me durgā bhālaṃ śrībhavagehinī ||5||
verses.en: She is the Great Vidyā, of the very form of Brahman, the essence of all knowledges. May Durgā guard my head; may Śrī‑Bhavagehinī guard my forehead.
singable: Durgā, guard my head; my brow,

verses.sa:
त्रिपुरा भ्रुयुगे पातु शर्वाणी पातु नासिकाम् ।
चक्षुषी चण्डिका पातु श्रोत्रे निलसरस्वती ॥६॥
verses.it:
tripurā bhruyuge pātu śarvāṇī pātu nāsikām |
cakṣuṣī caṇḍikā pātu śrotre nilasarasvatī ||6||
verses.en: May Tripurā guard my brows; may Śarvaṇī (Pārvatī) guard my nose. May Caṇḍikā guard my eyes; may Nīla‑Sarasvatī guard my ears.

verses.sa:
मुखं सौम्यमुखी पातु ग्रीवां रक्षतु पार्वती ।
जिह्वां रक्षतु मे देवी जिह्वाललनभीषणा ॥७॥
verses.it:
mukhaṃ saumyamukhī pātu grīvāṃ rakṣatu pārvatī |
jihvāṃ rakṣatu me devī jihvālalanabhīṣaṇā ||7||
verses.en: May the gentle‑faced One protect my face; may Pārvatī guard my neck. May the goddess—terrifying with her lolling tongue—guard my tongue.
singable: guard, O goddess, this my tongue.

verses.sa:
वाग्देवी वदनं पातु वक्षः पातु महेश्वरी ।
बाहू महाभुजा पातु कराङ्गुलीः सुरेश्वरी ॥८॥
verses.it:
vāgdevī vadanaṃ pātu vakṣaḥ pātu maheśvarī |
bāhū mahābhujā pātu karāṅgulīḥ sureśvarī ||8||
verses.en: May Vāgdevī protect my speech and mouth; may Maheśvarī guard my chest. May the Mighty‑Armed One protect my arms, and the Queen of the gods protect my fingers.

verses.sa:
पृष्ठतः पातु भीमास्या कट्यां देवी दिगम्बरी ।
उदरं पातु मे नित्यं महाविद्या महोदरी ॥९॥
verses.it:
pṛṣṭhataḥ pātu bhīmāsyā kaṭyāṃ devī digambarī |
udaraṃ pātu me nityaṃ mahāvidyā mahodarī ||9||
verses.en: From behind may Bhīmāsyā protect; at the hips, the goddess Digambarī. May the Great Vidyā Mahodari always protect my abdomen.

verses.sa:
उग्रतारा महादेवी जङ्घोरू परिरक्षतु ।
गुदं मुष्कं च मेढ्रं च नाभिं च सुरसुन्दरी ॥१०॥
verses.it:
ugratārā mahādevī jaṅghorū parirakṣatu |
gudaṃ muṣkaṃ ca meḍhraṃ ca nābhiṃ ca surasundarī ||10||
verses.en: May the great goddess Ugratārā protect my shanks and thighs. May Ugratārā—Surasundarī—guard the anus, the scrotum, the phallus, and the navel.
singable: guard my shanks and both my thighs;

verses.sa:
पादाङ्गुलीः सदा पातु भवानी त्रिदशेश्वरी ।
रक्तमांसास्थिमज्जादीन् पातु देवी शवासना ॥११॥
verses.it:
pādāṅgulīḥ sadā pātu bhavānī tridaśeśvarī |
raktamāṃsāsthimajjādīn pātu devī śavāsanā ||11||
verses.en: May Bhavānī, queen of the gods, always protect my toes. May the goddess who is seated upon a corpse protect my blood, flesh, bones, marrow, and the rest.
singable: flesh and bone and marrow too.

verses.sa:
महाभयेषु घोरेषु महाभयनिवारिणी ।
पातु देवी महामाया कामाख्यापीठवासिनी ॥१२॥
verses.it:
mahābhayeṣu ghoreṣu mahābhayanivāriṇī |
pātu devī mahāmāyā kāmākhyāpīṭhavāsinī ||12||
verses.en: In great and terrible dangers, may the remover of great fear—Mahāmāyā, who dwells at the seat of Kāmākhyā—protect me.
singable: In great terrors, dire and vast,
she who ends the greatest fear—

verses.sa:
भस्माचलगता दिव्यसिंहासनकृताश्रया ।
पातु श्रीकालिकादेवी सर्वोत्पातेषु सर्वदा ॥१३॥
verses.it:
bhasmācalagatā divyasiṃhāsanakṛtāśrayā |
pātu śrīkālikādevī sarvotpāteṣu sarvadā ||13||
verses.en: Residing on the mountain of ash, enthroned upon the divine lion seat—may Śrī Kālika‑devī protect me from every calamity, always.
singable: On the mount of ash enthroned,

verses.sa:
रक्षाहीनं तु यत्स्थानं कवचेनापि वर्जितम् ।
तत्सर्वं सर्वदा पातु सर्वरक्षणकारिणी ॥१४॥
verses.it:
rakṣāhīnaṃ tu yatsthānaṃ kavacenāpi varjitam |
tatsarvaṃ sarvadā pātu sarvarakṣaṇakāriṇī ||14||
verses.en: And whatever place is left unprotected—omitted even by this armor—may the All‑Protecting Goddess guard all of that, at all times.

history.note: Claude first draft — all 14 verses, ~8-syllable lines.

lines: In the east, may Tārā keep me,
the Great One, guards me always.
Durgā, guard my head; my brow,
guard, O goddess, this my tongue.
guard my shanks and both my thighs;
flesh and bone and marrow too.
In great terrors, dire and vast,
she who ends the greatest fear—
On the mount of ash enthroned,
